step_by_step_reasoning: Based on the transcript: 1. Users (UserConsumerWebMobile) request personalized videos via API Gateway (ApiGateway) and play them via CloudFront (CloudFront). 2. API Gateway forwards requests to the Frontend Lambda (Lambda 3). 3. The Frontend Lambda pushes rendering jobs to the Ingestion SQS queue (SQS 6) and queries statuses from the Feedback SQS queue (SQS 7). 4. EC2 Spot Instances (EC2 8) pull jobs from SQS 6, read templates/segments from S3 (S3 11), upload rendered segments back to S3, and push status signals to SQS 7. 5. Spot Interruption notices are caught by CloudWatch (CloudWatch 10), which triggers a Spot Handler Lambda (Lambda 4) to drain the target EC2 instance. 6. Scaling is automated: Step Functions (StepFunctions 9) triggers a Scaling Lambda (Lambda 5) every 15 seconds to check CloudWatch metrics and adjust the EC2 Auto Scaling Group.

graph.name: Sunday Sky: Personalized Video Rendering at Scale
graph.notes: Sunday Sky's Rendering as a Service (RAS) generates personalized videos at scale. User requests via API Gateway trigger a frontend Lambda, which pushes jobs to an SQS queue. EC2 Spot Instances pull jobs, render segments, and upload them to S3, from where they are served via CloudFront. Spot interruptions are managed via CloudWatch events triggering a drain Lambda. Scaling is orchestrated by Step Functions triggering a metric-checking Lambda.

nodes.name: Viewer
nodes.notes: Viewer requesting and playing personalized videos.

nodes.name: Amazon CloudFront
nodes.notes: Caches and delivers video segments from S3 origin to viewers.

nodes.name: API Gateway
nodes.notes: Receives video rendering requests with usage plans and API keys.

nodes.name: RAS Frontend Lambda
nodes.notes: Validates requests, pushes jobs to SQS, and pulls statuses.

nodes.name: Spot Handler Lambda
nodes.notes: Triggered by spot interruptions to drain EC2 instances.

nodes.name: Autoscaling Lambda
nodes.notes: Checks CloudWatch metrics and triggers ASG scaling.

nodes.name: Status Feedback Queue
nodes.notes: Receives quick updates from rendering engines.

nodes.name: Rendering ASG Spot Instances
nodes.notes: Auto Scaling Group of spot instances rendering video segments.

nodes.name: Step Functions Orchestrator
nodes.notes: Runs every 15 seconds to trigger scaling checks.

nodes.name: Amazon CloudWatch
nodes.notes: Gathers engine metrics and handles spot interruption notices.

nodes.name: Video Segments Bucket
nodes.notes: Stores uploaded video segments and playlist files.

edges.notes: Engines pull jobs from queue when they have capacity.
Pushes response to status feedback queue.
Signals engine to stop taking jobs and drain.
